items.title: Matplotlib
items.description: Once you decide on bar, line, scatter, or histogram, Matplotlib provides ax.bar, ax.plot, ax.scatter, and ax.hist to draw static figures. This topic chooses; Matplotlib implements.

items.title: Seaborn
items.description: Seaborn wraps statistical views—boxplot, heatmap, histplot, pairplot—on Matplotlib. Use this topic to pick box vs scatter vs heatmap; use Seaborn for DataFrame-oriented defaults.

items.title: Plotly
items.description: Plotly Express maps the same chart families to interactive HTML. Chart-type rules are identical; Plotly adds hover, zoom, and dashboard embedding without changing which family fits the question.

items.title: Common distributions
items.description: Histogram shape—bell, skewed, discrete spikes—connects to Bernoulli, Poisson, Gaussian, and related models. This topic says use a histogram for univariate numeric data; Common distributions explains what shape might mean statistically.
